def: s.f. Moving about, strolling, stroll, ramble, walk, taking the air, airing, perambulation, excursion, tour, travels; recreation, amusement; scene, view, spectacle, landscape; perusal (of a book, &c.); a sally (of wit, &c.), a jest
more: n. f. 1. Perambulation; excursion; tour (سفر). 2. ( sail sapāṭā, sail tamāshā ) Taking the air; airing; walk; recreation. 3. Fair; festival; marketing. Muflisī, aur hāṭ kī sail! Prov. Poverty goes a marketing! 4. Jest ( tamāsha 1). Kyā sair huī hai! 5. Perusal; reading. Kitāb kī sair . 6. A spectacle; view; scene; landscape. sair k. , v. a. 1. To take the air. 2. To make an excursion, etc. 3. To read; peruse. 4. To enjoy sights, etc. sair-gāh , n. f. 1. A place for recreation. 2. A place affording a good view. sair o shikār . Shooting and hunting.

s. f. Perambulation, excursion, tour, travels, taking the air, an airing, walk, recreation, amusement, moving about; perusal. سير کرنا sair karnā, 1. To take the air, travel, perambulate, rove, walk. 2. To read, to peruse. سير گاه sair-gāh, s. f. Place of perambulation, walk, passage, pass, thoroughfare.
subs: v.n. To take the air, to stroll, ramble, perambulate; to take amusement, to enjoy sights, to view or contemplate a beautiful landscape; to make an excursion, &c.; to read, peruse
s.f. Place of perambulation, walk, passage, thoroughfare; place of recreation; a place affording a good view or prospect
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَیر (sayr), from Arabic سَيْر (sayr).

def: n. m. Octroi. sāir kharch , Contingent charges.
more: 1. s. m. The whole. 2. The remainder. 3. part. act. (of سير) Going, walking, wandering. 4. Tax, duty, levied on personal property.

def: s.m. A money-changer, &c. ( = ṣarrāf); an examiner, or a skilful judge, of coins, jewels, and precious stones; — a practiser of art or artifice or cunning, a deceiver, a cheat.
s.m. A money-changer, &c. ( = ṣarrāf); an examiner, or a skilful judge, of coins, jewels, and precious stones; — a practiser of art or artifice or cunning, a deceiver, a cheat.
s.m. pl. (of sāʼir, — barbarous), Taxes (on personal property), transit duties.
outing, excursion

یہ راس رنگ یہ میل ملن اک ہاتھ میں چاند اک میں سورج

ye rās raṅg ye mel milan ik hāth mẽ cānd ik mẽ sūraj
ik rāt kā mauj mazā sārā ik din kā sair sapāṭā hai

wety: From سیر (ser, “stroll, walk”) + سپاٹا (“run, spurt”).

s.m. See سئيس saʼīs, and سائيس sāʼīs.

def: s.m. A groom, horse-keeper.
more: n. m. lit . a nobleman. A groom; a horse-keeper. Saīsoṅ kā kāl, munshiyoṅ kī bohtāt . Prov. Grooms scarce, clerks in plenty. sā'isī ; Illit. saīsī , n. f. The business of a groom. Saīsī ilam daryāo hai . Illit. Prov. The groom's knowledge is as deep as the sea. (There are mysteries in every trade).

s.m. corr. of śaithilya, q.v.
s.m. Looseness, laxity; flaccidity; slackness, remissness; dilatoriness, inattention; weakness; cowardice smallness; relaxation of rule or of connexion.

more: v. a. To take care of, to keep carefully, to husband.

v.t. To lay by; to lay in; to provide, &c.

s.m. Coldness, cold, frigidity.
s.m. = شيو śaiva, q.v.

adj. Relating or belonging to the god Śiva; — s.m. Name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects (the other two being Vaishṇavas and Śāktas); a worshipper of Śiva, a Śivite; — the plant Vallisneria octandra.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शिव (śiva).

s.m. = śaivāl, q.v.

more: n. m. 1. A hunter; fowler; fisherman (اﮩيرَي). 2. ( Poet .) Heart-ravisher.
(adj. superl. of صيد Heb. Hunting) s. m. A hunter, fowler, fisherman.

ety: ṣaiyād, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Hunting, fowling, shooting, trapping or snaring game, &c.
s.m. A great or frequent traveller, an itinerant; a pilgrim; a travelled man; — adj. Travelling, peregrinating, itinerant.
more: n. m. 1. A traveller. 2. A travelled man; a man of the world. saiyāh' , adj. Travelling; peregrinating.
(adj. superl. from سيح) s. m. A traveller, a pilgrim: adj. Travelling much, peregrinating.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَیَّاح (sayyāh), from Arabic سَيَّاح (sayyāḥ).

def: s.f. Travelling, journeying; journey, voyage; pilgrimage.
more: n. f. Travelling; a journey; voyage.

def: adj. Flowing or running much, flowing rapidly, rapid.
more: adj. سيل flowed. ātish-i-saiyāl , Poet. n. f. 1. Liquid fire. 2. Wine.
adj. (superl. from سيل To flow) Flowing rapidly like a torrent, rapid.

ety: for A. سيالة saiyālat, fem. of saiyāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A torrent; a body or volume (of anything) poured out or forth.
Poet. n. m. Husband.
s.m. = sāʼīṅ, or sāṅʼīṅ, qq.v. s.m. = سانئي sāṅʼī, q.v. s.m.f. Second; — a second; match, equal

more: (adj. superl. from سير) s. m. A wanderer, a person taking the air or travelling for amusement, wandering, moving.

ety: for A. سيارة, saiyārat, fem. of saiyār, q.v.
def: s.m. A planet (P. plur. saiyāragān)
more: n. m. سير journeyed. A planet. saba saiyārah . Seven stars, as the sun, moon, etc.
(from سير) s. m. A planet. (Pers. plur. سيارگان saiyāra-gān ).
subs: The seven planets (the Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سیاره (sayyāra), from Arabic سَيَّارَة (sayyāra), itself an ellipsis of كَوْكَب سَيَّار (kawkab sayyār, “wandering star”).

ety: for A. ثيبة s̤aiyibat, for s̤aiyīb, orig. s̤awīb
def: s.f. A young woman who has consummated her marriage, or one that is not a virgin.
more: s. f. A young woman who has consummated her marriage, fœmina cujus periit virginitas.

def: s.m. A lord, ohief, prince; — any descendant of Mohammad, (esp.) a descendant of Husain (who was the grandson of Moham mad)
more: n. m. saidānī , n. f. سود became chief. 1. A descendant of Husain, the grandson of Mahammad . 2. The second of the four classes into which Mahomedans are divided. 3. The holy spirit of a Saiyad. Unke sir Saiyad āë haíṅ .

(verbal adj. from سود) s. m. 1. A lord, a prince. 2. A descendant of Ḥusain, who was the grandson of the prophet Muḥammad. سيد الانام saiyidu-l-anām (the prince of men and of all living creatures), سيد البشر saiyidu-l-bashar (the prince of mortals), سيد المرسلين يا انبيا saiyidu-l-mursalīn or -ambiyā (the head of the apostles, or prophets) are epithets of Muḥammad.
subs: 'Chief of the apostles, or prophets,' Mohammad
adj. & s.m. Born of a Saiyid; — a Saiyid's son.

ety: for A. سيدة saiyidat, fem. of سيد
def: s.f. The wife or daughter of a Saiyid; a lady, a princess.
s.f. A woman of the Saiyid caste; the wife or daughter of a saiyid.
s.m. An appellation of Africans; a negro.

s.m. The teak-tree, Tectona grandis (syn. sāgūn); — the plane-tree.

def: s.f. Preparation; dress, ornament, decoration; appearance, shape
more: s. f. Shape, ornament, appearance, preparation. سج دار saj-dār, Well-shaped, handsome. سجدهج saj-dhaj, s. f. Preparation and appearance.
subs: s.f. Preparation and appearance; figure; adorning, ornamentation; grace, elegance.

more: s. m. Rhyme, metre, cadence, poesy.
subs: s.m. One who speaks rhythmically; a rhymer; an eloquent speaker.

or sājhe-meṅ, adv. In partnership, jointly

s.f. Preparation, &c. ( = sajāwaṭ, q.v.); — price paid for accoutrements, or for belts and scabbards, &c.
s. f. 1. The price paid for making belts or scabbards. 2. Preparation.
Possessing or containing water, filled with or abounding in water, watery, wet, moist, humid.
more: adj. S. सज्जित Good (اچها 1, 2, 4—7). Ajī hazat! koī sijal māl nikālo! sijal banānā , v. a. 1. To furbish; polish. 2. To improve; set off.
adj. With water, watery, filled with or containing water.

ety: Prk. सज्जणो; S. सज्जनः, i.e. सत्+जनः
def: s.m. Lover, paramour, sweetheart, gallant; husband (cf. sajjan).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: s.m. Preparing, dressing, decorating, adorning.
more: n. m. S. सज्जन from सत् true, जन man 1. Husband; lord; master. 1. Mā, āë sājan le chale, Merā terā bas nahīṅ chaltā rī mā! Hin. Girls' song. O mother! now my spouse is come, he taketh me away, No power in me nor you our separation sad can stay! 2. Sājan! woh din kaunsā, jo sukh se lāë pīt, Ab dukh de niyāre bhaë, kaun gāoṅ kī rīt? My spouse! the day hath been when love to me brought gladness here, And now to go away and give me pain doth strange appear! 3. Kar le siṅgār chatar albelī! sājan ke ghar jānā hogā, Vahīṅ terā pīhar, vahīṅ terā sahurā, vahīṅ terā pīv nimānā hogā . Masty. Adorn yourself, gay sprightly nymph! to your lord's house you must go! Your father-in-law, and house, and tender spouse there you shall know. 2. A sweetheart; lover.
s. m. A sweetheart, a lover.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: v.t. To cause (a person) to prepare (a thing); to arrange, prepare, assort, methodize; to decorate, adorn; to mend
more: v. a. S. सज्ज 1. To assort; sort; settle ( tartīb k .). 2. To adorn ( ārāstah k .). Ūdālaṅhgā, jarad oḍhnā, paṅch raṅg āṅguṭlā dūṅgā! Tane upar-sī ḍhāl pharā-kar latte, ṭūm sajā dūṅgā . Rus. Wom. song. Mauve skirt, wrap yellow, boddice of five colors I'll bring you, A veil o'er you bedecked I'll throw, a fairy bright to view.
(caus. of سجنا sajnā ) v. a. To cause to prepare.
subs: v.t. To put into a state of preparation; to arrange, order, to set out; to adorn.

ety: said to be a Greek word arabicised
def: s.m. A mirror, a Chinese mirror; — spectacles; adj. Pure, clear.

def: Preparation, arrangement, order; adorning, decoration, ornament; contrivance.

s.f. Dressing, decking or adorning (oneself).

n. f. See tartīb , and ārāstagī , 3. saj-dhaj , n. f. 1. Figure (دَول 1). 2. Grace; elegance. Hans denā har dam nāz-bharā, dikhlānā saj dhaj shokhī kā, Har gālī misrī qand bharī, har ek qadam aṭkhelī kā, Dil shād kīyā, aur moh līyā, yeh joban pāyā Holī ne! Nazīr. With laughter constant, blandishments and piquant charms displayed, Abuse too showered as candy sweet, each step the wanton swayed The heart made glad, the senses tranced, such beauty Holī made!

ety: sajā(nā) + Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्यं, and (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Preparation, arrangement, order; adorning, decoration, ornament; contrivance.
s.f. (pl. of سجية sajīyat, v.n. fr. سجو 'to cover or conceal by darkness'), Nature, natural disposition or temper; faculty, quality.
more: s. plur. (of سجيّة from سجا To rest, continue) Disposition, quality, nature.

vocative plural of سَجْدَہ (sajda, “prostration”)

ety: Prk. सज्झओ; S. साह्य, or सह्य (rt. सह्)+कः
def: s.m. Association, company (in trade), partnership; relation, connection; — share (in a company); — adj. Joint
more: n. m. S. साहाय्य. 1. An association; a company; joint concern; community of sharers; partnership. 2. A share. 1. Sājhe kī Holī sab se bhalī . Prov. The carnival of Holi best in company. 2. Sakhī kī kamāī meṅ sab kā sājhā . Prov. In the generous man's earnings all have a share. 3. Sājhe kī mā Gaṅgā na pāve . Prov. The mother who hath joint-heirs left, of Ganges' healing virtues is bereft. (Each one leaves to. the others the expense of conveying her body to the Ganges and feasting the Brahmans). 4. Sājhā jorū aur khasam hīkā achchhā hai . Prov. The best partnership is that between man and wife. 5. Tel dāl kamlī kā sājhā! Prov. He brought a little oil, in the jack fruit he a share must have! 6. Sājhe kī haṅḍyā chaurāhe meṅ phūṭe . Prov. The partners' cooking pot is broken on the public cross-road. (When partners break their affairs become public). 7. Sājhā sadhe na bāp kā, saë rāse kī khān;…
subs: (-kā), Partnership to be dissolved; connection to be severed; — sājhā ćhūṭnā, Dissolution of partnership
v.n. To form an association or a company; to enter into partnership
or sājhe-meṅ, adv. In partnership, jointly
adv. As a partner or sharer in, with the eye of a partner, as if in co-parcenary.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. sājhin, or sājhan), Assistant, helpful; — associate, colleague; partner; coparcener; shareholder
more: n. m. A partner (in business); co-partner; co-parcener; shareholder. sājhiyoṅ kā iqrār-nāmah, sājhe kā kāgaz . A deed of partnership. sājhī h., sājhā k. , v. n. To enter into partnership; to form a company. sājhe, sājhe meṅ , adv. Jointly; in partnership. nafe o nuqsān kā sājhī , A partner in gain and loss.
subs: v.n. To be or become a partner, &c. ( = sājhā karnā, q.v.).

ety: act. part. of سجد 'to bend oneself down to the ground,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Prostrate in adoration; — one who prostrates himself in adoration or prayer, an adorer, a worshipper.
more: (part. act. of سجد Heb. and Chald. To humble or prostrate oneʼs self, to adore) 1. adj. Prostrate in adoration. 2. s. m. An adorer.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-shaped, graceful, handsome; well-dressed; decorated.
more: adj. 1. Well-shaped; handsome; elegant. 2. Well-dressed; stylish.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having life, endowed with life, living, alive; — a living creature.
adj. corr. of sa-jīv, q.v.

s.m. = sanjīvan, q.v. s.m. Bringing to life, animating; re-animating, life-restoring; — the act of living together; — a cluster of four houses, a quadrangle; — name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions.

ety: for A. سجية sajīyat, v.n. fr. سجو 'to cover or conceal by darkness,' &c.
def: s.m. Nature, disposition, temper (see the pl. sajāyā, which is the more common form).
s.f. An armed female, &c. (see sajja); — dress; decoration; apparatus, equipment, equipage, harness; armour, mail.
adj. Right (arm or hand).
adj. Covered, clothed, dressed, accoutred; ornamented, decorated; prepared, ready; armed, fortified; — s.m. An armed person, &c.

ety: intens. n. fr. سجد 'to prostrate oneself'
def: adj. & s.m. Prostrating oneself much or frequently (in prayer), bowing the head in adoration, adoring; — one who bows in adoration, &c.
more: adj. (superl. of سجد Heb. ) Adoring, bowing the body in adoration.

ety: for A. سجادة sajjādat; see sajjād
def: s.m. A carpet or mat on which a Mohammadan prostrates himself (and stands and sits) in prayer; place of adoration; mosque; altar
more: n. m. A mat or carpet on which Mahommedans pray; an altar.
(from سجد) s. m. A carpet, or mat on which Muhammadans kneel at prayers, an altar, a mosque.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Sitting upon the prayer-carpet'; a Mohammadan priest (when saying prayers); any religious ascetic of the Mohammadan faith; — the spiritual superior of a mosque or religious endowment (as distinguished from the Mutawallī or secular superintendent).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
more: n. m. S. सज्जन from सत true जन man. 1. A respectable person; a good or virtuous man; a gentleman. Rām rāj-abhishek suni hīye harkhe nar nārī, Lagī sumaṅgal sajan sab Bidhī anukul bichār . Rāmāyan. 2. A sweetheart; lover; husband. Sajan, tum jhūṭ mat bolo! Khudā ko sāṅch piyārā hai, Kahāvat hai baḍoṅ kī yūn, 'kadhī sāṅchā na hārā hai .' My husband, do not tell a lie! God loves the truth, you know, It hath been said of old 'the truthful ne'er hath come to woe.'
s. m. f. A respectable person, a sweetheart, a mistress.

def: s.m. Fastening on (clothes), dressing; putting on armour, arming, preparing; — a guard, sentry, picquet; a ferry (syn. ghāṭ).
s.f. = sajanī, q.v.

s.m. = sajjan, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
s.f. The act of dressing; decoration; ornamenting or caparisoning an elephant.
s.f. A virtuous woman; a respectable woman; a mistress, sweetheart; a female friend (syn. sahelī).
more: n. f. A female friend. 1. Uṭh rī! Holī ho rahī! tū kyā paṛī sove rī! Rain gaī to jāne de, sajnī, din mat khove rī! Song. Holī . Get up! 'tis Holī! this is not a time for sleep, So be 't the night is past, the day in revels keep! 2. Sun sun, sajnī, kaise kaṭegī rajnī! Pī par jāūṅ, khaṛī pachhtāūṅ, pāël merī bajnī . Song. Lāunī . Hear, hear, my dear! how ever shall I pass the night? If to my spouse I go I shall repent it quite, My anklets clink with every motion e'er so slight.

def: s.f. Natron, impure carbonate of soda, a kind of mineral alkali (used in India instead of soap for cleaning linen, &c.; syn. khār)
more: n. f. S. सर्ज्जो The Alkaline produce of a plant, rāj bhaṅg or rehe , q. v. ( Coroxylon Griffithū ) obtained from its ashes when burnt; fuller's earth. Three qualities are distinguished— choā-sajjī , the purest; hathā-sajjī , the second quality; and kara-sajjī , the most impure. All these are varieties of the barilla or soda of commerce.
s. f. A kind of mineral alkali, natron, impure carbonate of soda.

def: adj. & s.m. = saṅjhlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), The third (of four brothers, &c.); of the third rank or degree; — the third of four brothers.
more: s. m. Third brother of four or more.

ety: sāj˚ = Prk. सज्ज(इ) = S. सज्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. संज्
def: v.t. To prepare, to fit out; to dress, decorate, bedeck; to regulate; — v.n. ( = sajnā), To be prepared, &c.; to be accoutred; — to become, fit, suit.
more: 1. v. a. To prepare, dress, decorate, bedeck, regulate. 2. v. n. To become, fit, suit.

def: n. f. S. शाखा sub-division. 1. Season. 2. Harvest; crop.
more: n. f. S. साक्ष्य 1. The evidence of a witness. 2. Weight or credibility; authority. 2. Trust ( etebār , 1); mercantile or commercial credit. 4. Name; reputation; good report; credit. 1. Jisko āṅkh nahīṅ, usko sākh . Prov. No eyes have they to see, yet in their testimony all agree. 2. Kākā na rakkhe sākhā . Prov. Your old slave as your uncle fear, or else your reputation he 'll not spare. 3. Le-ke de chāhat nahīṅ, kiryā karat haiṅ lākh! Tulsī, aise jīv kī kahā kare koī sākh? Tulsī Dās. They take but like not to give back, a lakh of oaths they take, Tulsī! with such as these who e'er can bond or contract make? 4. Jag partīt biṭhāiye, rahiye sāṅche hoë! Jhūṭe nar kī sāṅch hī sākh na māne koë . Your credit in the world established firm, e'er truthful be! The false man's attestation will by no one trusted be. 5. Ijjat to nā bigaṛsī, dil meṅ dhīrī rākh! Jo yahāṅ se uṭh jāëgā bigaṛ jāëgī sākh .…

Terā neg dīyā sab manne, phir bhī tauṅ ropā sākhā . Rus. I've given you all your presents due and yet you wrangle and fight with me! 2. An epic; a ballad. 3. An epoch; era, esp. that of Sāli-vāhan commencing from A. D. 78. 4. (S. शाखा, H. sākhā-chār ) The genealogies of the bride and bridegroom's families recited at a Hin. marriage. sākhā ḍālnā yā k. , v. a. To quarrel; fight. sākhe-khor , Wom. n. m. A quarrelsome person.

ety: prob. Zend śakhta, rt. śac = S. शक्त, rt. शक्; but said to be another form of sanjīda, q.v.
def: adj. Hard, stiff, rigid, firm, fast; strong, solid; tight; constipated; obdurate, obstinate; stingy, sordid; wretched; difficult, arduous, troublesome; painful, grievous; severe, intense, vehement, violent; great, gross, excessive, extreme, egregious; austere, stern, harsh; very cruel, fell; — adv. Very, intensely, violently, severely, excessively, extremely, &c.
more: adj. 1. Hardy; flinty; adamantine; inflexible. Zamīn sakht aur āsmān dūr hai . Prov. The earth so hard, the sky so far. (Whither shall I fly?) 2. Difficult ( bhārī , 12, 13). Sakht kām hai . 3. Obdurate; stubborn; inexorable. 4. Hard-hearted; cruel; unfecling. Beṭā! ab tū mujh se sakhat ho gayā . Wom. 5. Rigid; strict; austere. Woh nambar dene meṅ baṛā sakht hai . 6. Severe; violent; grievous; harsh. 7. Great; gross ( bhārī , 19). 8. Stingy; close-fisted ( kanjūs ). 9. Inauspicious; unlucky. Sakht din. sakht , adv. Severely; violently; extremely, etc. sakht be-insāfī , Great injustice. sakht be-īmānī , Gross breach of trust. sakht zamīn , n. f. 1. Hard soil or ground. 2. Firm ground. sakht sust kahnā , v. a. To use harsh words; rail at ( āṛe hāth lenā , 2, 5.).

adj. 1. Hard, strong. 2. Difficult. 3. Obdurate. 4. Stingy. 5. Wretched, troublesome, violent, vehement, very cruel, fell, painful, austere, rigid, severe, harsh (to the ear). 6. adv. Very, intensely, violently, extremely. سخت انداز saḵẖt-andāz, An archer. سخت گوي saḵẖt-go-ī, s. f. Harshness of language, abuse. سخت گير saḵẖt-gīr, Holding fast (an animal of chase). سخت گيري saḵẖt-gīr-ī, s. f. Extortion, taking by force.
subs: adj. Of austere countenance, stern-faced, sour-looking

ety: perf. part. of saḵẖtan; see sāḵẖt
def: part. adj. Made, formed; artificial, counterfeited, fictitious, false, feigned; surreptitious

( Law .) The act of one shall be held as the act of the other. be-sākhtah , adj. 1. Unaffected; not forced; natural. 2. Simple ( sādah , 2, 3.) Ajab ek ālam thā be-sākhtah, Ki ālam thā ek us pe jāṅ-bākhtah . Mīr Hasan. A world of beauty strange upsprung, A world devoted 'bout her hung. 3. Unpremeditated; unprepared; impromptu; extempore . be-sākhtah , adv. 1. Without preparation; spontaneously. 2. See dafatan .
part. pass. (from ساختن) Made, formed; artificial, fictitious, stiff.
subs: and sāḵẖta-pardākhta, part. adj. & s.m. Done and effected; anything done or had done, act, deed
adj. & adv. Natural, unprepared, unaffected; simple; unpremeditated, impromptu, extempore; without preparation, spontaneously, on the spur of the moment.

def: s.f. Make, &c. ( = sāḵẖt, q.v.); art, design; effort; machination. s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.

s.f. Hardness, stiffness, rigidity, firmness; tightness; stinginess; obduracy, obstinacy; intenseness, intensity, vehemence, severity; harshness, asperity; sternness, austereness; violence, atrocity; cruelty; grievance, hardship; adversity, indigence, distress, difficulty, evil, calamity
more: n. f. 1. Hardness; firmness; rigidity; inflexibility. Iske peṭ meṅ baṛī sakhtī hai . 2. Obduracy; stubbornness. 3. Hard-heartedness; cruelty. 4. Harshness; severity; asperity. 5. Violence ( tāddī , 1—4). 6. Strictness; rigidity; austerity. 7. Poverty; indigence. 8. Distress ( taṅgī , 3). sakhtī se , adv. Harshly; severely, etc. sakhtī se pesh ānā , v. a. 1. To treat harshly. 2. To be strict or rigorous. sakhtī k. , v. a. To use force or violence, etc.

s. f. 1. Hardness, firmness, harshness, intenseness, vehemence, severity, asperity, atrocity, grievance, hardship. 2. Obduracy. 3. Stinginess, indigence. 4. Distress, difficulty. سختي کوپينا saḵẖtī ko pīnā, (dakh.) See برداشت کرنا bar-dāsht karnā under برداشت.
subs: (-se), To use force or violence (towards), treat with harshness or severity, &c.

s. f. (dakh.) See سکهي sakhī. ¶ sukī, See سکهي sukhī.

ety: for A. ساقٍ sāqiṉ, act. part. of سقي 'to give (water, &c.
def: s.m. One who gives to drink, cup-bearer, page; (in India) one who gets ready and brings the ḥuqqa
more: Poet. n. m. سقي gave him to drink. 1. A cup-bearer; a cup-boy. Take hai zāhid sharāb-i-gulgūṅ, huā hai dil bhī kharāb ādhā, Khilā de, sāqī, balā se isko ḍabo-ke tū bhī kabāb ādhā! Saiyad. The saint stares at the wine, his heart with foulness is half-choked, Give him, ne'er mind, cup boy! just half the roast in wine full soaked. 2. A sweetheart; one beloved; love. Mai bhī hai, mīnā bhī hai, sāgar bhī hai, sāqī nahīṅ! Jī meṅ ātā hai lagā deṅ āg mai-khāne ko ham . Goyā. Wine too is here, wine-cup and flask, the cup-bearer is not! I could this public house with fire destroy and bring to naught. sāqiyā! O cup bearer! O cup boy!
(part. act. of سقي Heb. To irrigate) s. m. A cupbearer, a page. (Plur. ساقيان sāḳiyān ). ساقيا sāḳiyā, O cupbearer!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَاقِی (sāqī), from Arabic سَاقٍ (sāqin).

def: (act. part. of ثقب 'to pierce'), adj. lit. 'Penetrating'; shining brightly, glistening, gleaming very brightly, brilliant, splendid; famous, exalted, sublime, high.

ساقی گری کی شرم کرو آج ورنہ ہم
ہر شب پیا ہی کرتے ہیں مے جس قدر ملے
sāqī-garī kī śarm karo āj varnā ham
har śab piyā hī karte ha͠i may jis qadar mile

uphold the shame (the honor) of cupbearing today(,) else we
each night do drink wine of whichever quantity we (can) get
wety: From ساقی گر (sāqīgar, “cupbearer”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: S. सङ्कुचित+कः, or perf. part. of sakućnā
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrowed, compressed, tightened, tight; — shrivelled up, shrunk; closed, bound; abashed, ashamed; intimidated, awe-stricken ( = sakućāyā).
s.f. Shrinking; bashfulness; apprehensiveness; &c. (see sakuć).
v.t. To cause to shrink; to compress, to tighten; to abash; to frighten intimidate, overawe; — v.n. = sakućnā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To shrink; to be shy, bashful, modest. 2. To be frightened; to hesitate.
v. n. To be abashed, to be afraid. v. a. To abash.

def: adj. = sakućāyā; — s.f. = sakućāʼī or sakuć.

part. adj. (f. sakućāʼī) = sakućā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrowed, compressed, tightened, tight; — shrivelled up, shrunk; closed, bound; abashed, ashamed; intimidated, awe-stricken ( = sakućāyā).
v.n. To shrink, contract, become narrow or tight; to shrink (from), be afraid (of), be apprehensive (of), to fear; to hesitate; to be abashed, be shy, or bashful.
more: v. n. To fear, to be afraid of, to be in awe of, to be abashed, to apprehend, to be apprehensive.

def: adj. & s.m. Having a family; belonging to a noble family, of good lineage or stock; belonging to the same family; — a family man; — a kinsman; — a kind of fish (cf. saur).
more: n. S. शकुल A kind of fish. (?)

def: s.m. A kind of fish (cf. saur)
more: n. m. A sect of Brahmans. shukl-paksh ; H. shukal-pachch . Opp. of krishn-paksh , n. f. The light fortnight ( sudī ).
s. (dakh.) Polish, furbishing ( Harris ): see صيقل ṣaiḳal. شکل کرنا shikal karnā, To polish, burnish, furbish. شکل گر shikal-gar, Polisher, armourer, burnisher, furbisher.
subs: (˚la+ar˚), s.m. A species of the gilt-head, Sparus emarginatus
s.m. A kind of fish, the gilt-head.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَکْل (šakl), borrowed from Arabic شَكْل (šakl).

def: s.m. corr. of شاقول shāqūl, q.v. s.m. A plummet ( = sāhul, sahūl, sahol).
more: n. m. Cor. of T. shāqūl . A plumbline.

def: s.m. One of the same family and name ( = sa-gotra); a distant relation; a kinsman.
more: n. All the relations and connections of whatever degree. sakulya nevtā , n. A general invitation including the whole fraternity.

def: s.m. An omen ( = sagun, shugūn).
